# 'log short -w' filters branches to the current worktree.
# Full stacks are shown if any branch in the stack
# is checked out in the current worktree.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# Create two stacks:
# Stack 1: main -> feat1 -> feat2
# Stack 2: main -> feat3 -> feat4

# Stack 1
git add feat1.txt
gs bc feat1 -m 'Add feat1'
git add feat2.txt
gs bc feat2 -m 'Add feat2'

# Stack 2
gs bco main
git add feat3.txt
gs bc feat3 -m 'Add feat3'
git add feat4.txt
gs bc feat4 -m 'Add feat4'

# Create a worktree for feat1 (part of stack 1)
git worktree add ../wt1 feat1

# In the main repo, we are on feat4 (stack 2).
# -w should show only stack 2 (the current worktree's stack).
gs ls -w
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ls-main-repo.txt

# In wt1, we are on feat1 (stack 1).
# -w should show only stack 1.
cd ../wt1
gs ls -w
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ls-wt1.txt

# Without -w, -a shows all branches.
# Use regex matching for the worktree path because its separator
# can differ between platforms.
gs ls -a
stderr '  ┏━□ feat2'
stderr '┏━┻■ feat1 ◀'
stderr '┃ ┏━□ feat4 \[wt:.*repo\]'
stderr '┣━┻□ feat3'
stderr 'main'

-- repo/feat1.txt --
feat1
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feat2
-- repo/feat3.txt --
feat3
-- repo/feat4.txt --
feat4
-- golden/ls-main-repo.txt --
  ┏━■ feat4 ◀
┏━┻□ feat3
main
-- golden/ls-wt1.txt --
  ┏━□ feat2
┏━┻■ feat1 ◀
main
